p6_note: Precision@K only. Recall is UNKNOWN: total true duplicate pairs in 20,685 datasets was never labelled.

check_stdout: │ It seems that you have a classification problem with at least one class with │
│ fewer than 100 examples in the test set. In this case, using                 │
│ train_test_split may not be a good idea because of high variability in the   │
│ scores obtained on the test set. We suggest three options to tackle this     │
│ challenge: you can increase test_size, collect more data, or use skore's     │
│ CrossValidationReport with the `cv_splitter` parameter of your choice.       │
│ It seems that you have a classification problem with a high class imbalance. │
│ In this case, using train_test_split may not be a good idea because of high  │
│ variability in the scores obtained on the test set. To tackle this challenge │
p7_skore.interpretation: High accuracy proves the KMeans labels are learnable/reproducible from embeddings. It does NOT prove the themes are the 'right' themes -- the labels were produced by the same embeddings (circular). Treat as a STABILITY signal only.

regime_b_review_band.sampling: stratified across cosine bands 0.86-1.00, identical titles excluded, seed=0

p6_human_review.framing: Model FLAGS likely duplicates for human review; it does NOT auto-delete. Precision only; recall unknown.
